description: Genomic Identification of Significant Targets in Cancer (version 2.0.23)
author: Jen Dobson, Rameen Beroukhim, Gad Getz
plan:
## GISTIC_2.0 GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
GISTIC_2.0 is a GenePattern module that Genomic Identification of Significant Targets in Cancer (version 2.0.23)

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 22 parameters:
- Required: refgene.file, seg.file, gene.gistic, confidence.level, run.broad.analysis, focal.length.cutoff, arm.peel, gene.collapse.method
- Optional: markers.file, maxspace, array.list.file, cnv.file, amplifications.threshold, deletions.threshold, join.segment.size, q-value.threshold, remove.X, cap.val, max.sample.segs, sample.center, output.prefix, logdat

### 3. Command Line
```
gp_gistic2_from_seg -b . -seg <seg.file> <markers.file> <maxspace> -alf <array.list.file> -cnv <cnv.file> -refgene <refgene.file> <amplifications.threshold> <deletions.threshold> <join.segment.size> <q-value.threshold> <remove.X> <cap.val> -conf <confidence.level> -genegistic <gene.gistic> -broad <run.broad.analysis> -brlen <focal.length.cutoff> <max.sample.segs> -armpeel <arm.peel> <sample.center> -gcm <gene.collapse.method> <output.prefix> -smallmem 1 -savegene 1 -genepattern 1 -twosides 0 -saveseg 0 -savedata 0 -arb 1 -peak_type robust -smalldisk 0 -v 0 <logdat>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/docker-gistic\:0.13

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: refgene.file
parameters.description: The reference genome file input provides cytoband and gene location information.

parameters.name: seg.file
parameters.description: The segmentation file contains the segmented data for all the samples identified by GLAD, CBS, or some other segmentation algorithm. (See GLAD file format in the Genepattern file formats documentation.) It is a six column, tab-delimited file with an optional first line identifying the columns. Positions are in base pair units.

parameters.name: markers.file
parameters.description: The markers file identifies the marker names and positions of the markers in the original dataset (before segmentation). It is a three column, tab-delimited file with an optional header. If not already, markers are sorted by genomic position.

parameters.name: maxspace
parameters.description: Set maxspace to generate pseudo-markers instead of creating a markers file. The param defines the maximum allowed spacing between pseudo-markers, in bases. Segments that contain fewer than this number of markers are joined to the neighboring segment that is closest in copy number. When 'markers file' is set 'maxspace' is ignored. When neither are set, generate pseudo-markers, with maxspace\=10,000.

parameters.name: array.list.file
parameters.description: The array list file is an optional file identifying the subset of samples to be used in the analysis. It is a one column file with an optional header. The sample identifiers listed in the array list file must match the sample names given in the segmentation file.

parameters.name: cnv.file
parameters.description: Specifies germline CNV (or other highly variant) regions to exclude from significance analysis. There are two forms for the cnv file\: one allows CNVs to be identified by marker names in a single column; the other identifies excluded regions using their genomic location.

parameters.name: gene.gistic
parameters.description: Flag indicating that the gene GISTIC algorithm should be used to calculate the significant regions of deletion using gene locations instead of a marker locations.

parameters.name: amplifications.threshold
parameters.description: Threshold for copy number amplifications. Regions with a log2 ratio above this value are considered amplified.

parameters.name: deletions.threshold
parameters.description: Threshold for copy number deletions. Regions with a log2 ratio below the negative of this value are considered deletions.

parameters.name: join.segment.size
parameters.description: Smallest number of markers to allow in segments from the segmented data. Segments that contain a number of markers less than or equal to this number are joined to the neighboring segment that is closest in copy number.

parameters.name: q-value.threshold
parameters.description: Maximum false discovery rate for a region to be considered significant.

parameters.name: remove.X
parameters.description: Flag indicating whether to remove data from the X-chromosome before analysis.

parameters.name: cap.val
parameters.description: Minimum and maximum cap values on analyzed data. Regions with a log2 ratio greater than the cap are set to the cap value; regions with a log2 ratio less than -cap value are set to -cap.

parameters.name: confidence.level
parameters.description: Confidence level used to calculate the region containing a driver.

parameters.name: run.broad.analysis
parameters.description: Flag indicating whether an additional broad-level analysis should be performed.

parameters.name: focal.length.cutoff
parameters.description: Threshold used to distinguish broad from focal events, given in units of fraction of chromosome arm.

parameters.name: max.sample.segs
parameters.description: Maximum number of segments allowed for a sample in the input data. Samples with more segments than this threshold are excluded from the analysis.

parameters.name: arm.peel
parameters.description: Whether to perform arm level peel off. This helps separate peaks which cleans up noise.

parameters.name: sample.center
parameters.description: Method for centering each sample prior to the GISTIC analysis.

parameters.name: gene.collapse.method
parameters.description: Method for reducing marker-level copy number data to the gene-level copy number data in the gene tables. Markers contained in the gene are used when available, otherwise the flanking marker or markers are used.

parameters.name: output.prefix
parameters.description: The prefix for the output file name

parameters.name: logdat
parameters.description: The '-logdat' param to apply log2 ratio for SNP arrays, default\=autodetect
